summary: gpt-sdk postinstall beacons host, user, AD-domain, and CI repository identifiers to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js collects extensive installer-side identifiers and sends them via HTTP GET to http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping. Collected fields include hostname and reverse-DNS FQDN, Active Directory domain (USERDNSDOMAIN), USERDOMAIN, LOGONSERVER, USERNAME, USERPROFILE, proxy environment, presence of OneDrive/VPN/ZScaler/Netskope, current working directory, npm registry, and CI repository slugs across multiple providers (GITHUB_REPOSITORY, CIRCLE_*, CI_PROJECT_PATH, BITBUCKET_REPO_FULL_NAME, TRAVIS_REPO_SLUG, BUILD_REPOSITORY_URI, JENKINS_URL, CI_SERVER_URL). The package's main entry (index.js) exports an empty object, so the package provides no functionality and exists solely to deliver the beacon. The name 'gpt-sdk' impersonates an OpenAI/GPT SDK, baiting accidental installs. The collected fields go well beyond an anonymous install counter and are precisely the identifiers used to fingerprint corporate developer machines and CI systems for follow-on targeting.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
